title: Remove unconditional --dangerously-skip-permissions and replace blanket skip with a scoped --allowedTools allowlist
source: OWASP LLM Top 10 2025 — LLM06: Excessive Agency; Anthropic Claude CLI --allowedTools/--permission-mode docs. Found PROM-071 workaround in code.
description: delegate.py:1789-1791 unconditionally appends `--dangerously-skip-permissions` to every Morty subprocess, and the centralized env-gated guard `should_skip_permissions()` is dead-coded behind `if False`. This means every delegated agent runs with unrestricted tool access regardless of CTO_ALLOW_SKIP_PERMISSIONS, the exact Excessive Agency anti-pattern (OWASP LLM06). The workaround was added because Morty's stall on interactive permission prompts — the correct fix is least-privilege tool scoping, not blanket bypass.
proposed_changes: Delete the unconditional `cmd.append('--dangerously-skip-permissions')` at delegate.py:1789 and the dead `if False and should_skip_permissions(...)` branch. Replace with `--permission-mode acceptEdits` (non-interactive, no blanket bypass) plus a per-role `--allowedTools` allowlist defined in a new `ROLE_TOOL_ALLOWLISTS` dict in security_utils.py (e.g. backend-morty: Read,Write,Edit,Bash(git*),mcp__cto-orchestrator__*; architect-morty: Read,Grep,Glob only). Add `cmd.extend(['--allowedTools', ','.join(allowed)])`. Keep `should_skip_permissions()` as the only path to full skip, gated on CTO_ALLOW_SKIP_PERMISSIONS, and audit-log every invocation. This unblocks non-interactive Morty's without granting universal agency.
